questions.stem:
—I ______ the book already, so I ______ it to the library yesterday.
—Great! Now you can borrow a new one.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空后有already（已经），是现在完成时的标志词，强调过去动作对现在的影响（已经读完了），用have read；第二空后有yesterday（昨天），是明确的过去时间状语，只能用一般过去时returned。【技巧】口诀：already/ever/never用现完，yesterday/last week用过去。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my classmate who won the first prize.
questions.explanation: 【解析】定语从句修饰先行词the boy（人），且关系词在从句中作主语，故用who。which指物；whom指人但只能作宾语；whose表示"谁的"，在从句中作定语。【技巧】先行词是人且作主语，关系代词用who；先行词是物，用which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new bridge near our school ______ by the workers last year.
questions.explanation: 【解析】主语the bridge与build是被动关系（桥被建造），时间状语last year表示过去，故用一般过去时的被动语态was built。A是现在时被动；C是主动语态；D是现在完成时主动语态。【技巧】被动语态结构：be + 过去分词，时态看be动词变化。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: I don't know ______ he will come to the party or not. Let's call him to confirm.
questions.explanation: 【解析】宾语从句中有or not时，通常只用whether（whether...or not是固定搭配），if不能与or not直接连用。that引导陈述句宾从；what在从句中需充当成分（主语/宾语），而本句不缺成分。【技巧】whether vs if：有or not选whether；介词后、不定式前、主语从句表语从句同位语从句都用whether。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The weather is ______ today. Let's go for a picnic in the park.
questions.explanation: 【解析】pleasant意为"宜人的、舒适的"，符合后句"去野餐"的语境。terrible（糟糕的）、awful（极坏的）与语境相反；boring（无聊的）通常形容事物无趣，不形容天气。【技巧】根据上下文逻辑选择：去野餐→天气好→pleasant。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 【解析】-ing形容词（exciting）修饰物，表示"令人...的"；-ed形容词（excited）修饰人，表示"感到...的"。第一空修饰news（消息），用exciting；第二空修饰everyone（人），用excited。【技巧】口诀："令人"-ing，"感到"-ed；物用-ing，人用-ed。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: As the monitor, you should learn how to ______ the problems between classmates.
questions.explanation: 【解析】deal with意为"处理、应对"，符合语境"处理同学间的问题"。look for（寻找）；take off（起飞/脱下）；put on（穿上/上演）。【技巧】problem通常与solve/deal with搭配；look for强调寻找过程，find强调找到结果。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect waste paper and plastic bottles.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______. They didn't want to spend time on it. However, our teacher explained that small actions could make a big (9)______. Slowly, we began to change our minds.

My classmate Tom came up with a good (10)______. He suggested setting up different bins for different types of waste. This made the sorting work much (11)______. By the end of the month, we had collected over 500 kilos of recyclable materials. Everyone felt proud when we saw the (12)______ of our hard work.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据后句"They didn't want to spend time on it"（不想花时间）可知，学生们一开始认为这件事"无聊/枯燥"，选boring。interesting（有趣的）、necessary（必要的）、important（重要的）都与"不想做"的态度相反。【技巧】完形填空要注意上下文情感态度的一致性，后文的However表示转折，说明前文态度消极。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect waste paper and plastic bottles.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______. They didn't want to spend time on it. However, our teacher explained that small actions could make a big (9)______. Slowly, we began to change our minds.

My classmate Tom came up with a good (10)______. He suggested setting up different bins for different types of waste. This made the sorting work much (11)______. By the end of the month, we had collected over 500 kilos of recyclable materials. Everyone felt proud when we saw the (12)______ of our hard work.
questions.explanation: 【解析】固定搭配make a difference意为"有影响、起作用"。老师解释小行动也能产生大影响，说服学生参与。make a mistake（犯错误）；make a decision（做决定）；make a difficulty（制造困难）都不符合语境。【技巧】积累常考固定搭配：make a difference（有影响）、make progress（取得进步）、make sense（有意义）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect waste paper and plastic bottles.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______. They didn't want to spend time on it. However, our teacher explained that small actions could make a big (9)______. Slowly, we began to change our minds.

My classmate Tom came up with a good (10)______. He suggested setting up different bins for different types of waste. This made the sorting work much (11)______. By the end of the month, we had collected over 500 kilos of recyclable materials. Everyone felt proud when we saw the (12)______ of our hard work.
questions.explanation: 【解析】come up with an idea是固定搭配，意为"想出一个主意"。后文"suggested setting up different bins"（建议设置不同垃圾桶）具体说明了这个主意。excuse（借口）；example（例子）；opinion（观点）。【技巧】come up with常与idea/plan/solution搭配；excuse通常与make/give搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect waste paper and plastic bottles.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______. They didn't want to spend time on it. However, our teacher explained that small actions could make a big (9)______. Slowly, we began to change our minds.

My classmate Tom came up with a good (10)______. He suggested setting up different bins for different types of waste. This made the sorting work much (11)______. By the end of the month, we had collected over 500 kilos of recyclable materials. Everyone felt proud when we saw the (12)______ of our hard work.
questions.explanation: 【解析】设置不同垃圾桶分类，应该是让分类工作"更容易"（easier），而不是更难或更慢。faster（更快）通常形容速度，不形容工作难度。【技巧】上下文逻辑：good idea→积极结果→easier。注意much修饰比较级，表示"...得多"。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a recycling project. The purpose was to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect waste paper and plastic bottles.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______. They didn't want to spend time on it. However, our teacher explained that small actions could make a big (9)______. Slowly, we began to change our minds.

My classmate Tom came up with a good (10)______. He suggested setting up different bins for different types of waste. This made the sorting work much (11)______. By the end of the month, we had collected over 500 kilos of recyclable materials. Everyone felt proud when we saw the (12)______ of our hard work.
questions.explanation: 【解析】result意为"结果"，see the result of our hard work表示"看到我们努力的结果"，与前文"收集了500多公斤可回收物"对应。reason（原因）；cause（起因）；problem（问题）。【技巧】result表示结果（中性/积极），consequence常指后果（消极），outcome指最终 outcome。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Smart homes are becoming more popular in modern cities. A smart home uses technology to make life easier and more comfortable. For example, you can control the lights, temperature, and even the refrigerator with your smartphone (智能手机).

One amazing feature is the smart assistant. It can understand your voice commands. If you say "It's hot in here," the assistant will automatically turn on the air conditioner. Some systems can even learn your habits. They know when you usually get up, so they prepare your coffee and open the curtains at the right time.

However, smart homes also have challenges. The equipment is expensive, and not everyone can afford it. Also, some people worry about privacy. If hackers (黑客) break into the system, they might control your home or steal your personal information.

Despite these concerns, experts believe smart homes will become common in the future. As technology improves and costs drop, more families will enjoy the convenience of smart living.
questions.stem: What can people use to control devices in a smart home according to the passage?
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节理解题。根据第一段最后一句"you can control the lights, temperature, and even the refrigerator with your smartphone"可知，人们可以用智能手机控制智能家居设备。【技巧】细节题要找准定位句，通常出现在文章开头或段落首句，注意题干关键词control devices对应原文control the lights...
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Smart homes are becoming more popular in modern cities. A smart home uses technology to make life easier and more comfortable. For example, you can control the lights, temperature, and even the refrigerator with your smartphone (智能手机).

One amazing feature is the smart assistant. It can understand your voice commands. If you say "It's hot in here," the assistant will automatically turn on the air conditioner. Some systems can even learn your habits. They know when you usually get up, so they prepare your coffee and open the curtains at the right time.

However, smart homes also have challenges. The equipment is expensive, and not everyone can afford it. Also, some people worry about privacy. If hackers (黑客) break into the system, they might control your home or steal your personal information.

Despite these concerns, experts believe smart homes will become common in the future. As technology improves and costs drop, more families will enjoy the convenience of smart living.
questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage about smart assistants?
options: A. They can only understand simple words.
C. They can adapt to users' lifestyles.
D. They are difficult to install.
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断题。根据第二段"Some systems can even learn your habits. They know when you usually get up..."可知，智能助手能学习用户习惯，即适应用户的生活方式（adapt to users' lifestyles）。A、B选项过于绝对；D选项文中未提及。【技巧】推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能过度推断，也不能选择文中明确陈述的内容（那是细节题）。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Smart homes are becoming more popular in modern cities. A smart home uses technology to make life easier and more comfortable. For example, you can control the lights, temperature, and even the refrigerator with your smartphone (智能手机).

One amazing feature is the smart assistant. It can understand your voice commands. If you say "It's hot in here," the assistant will automatically turn on the air conditioner. Some systems can even learn your habits. They know when you usually get up, so they prepare your coffee and open the curtains at the right time.

However, smart homes also have challenges. The equipment is expensive, and not everyone can afford it. Also, some people worry about privacy. If hackers (黑客) break into the system, they might control your home or steal your personal information.

Despite these concerns, experts believe smart homes will become common in the future. As technology improves and costs drop, more families will enjoy the convenience of smart living.
questions.stem: The underlined word "hackers" in Paragraph 3 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测题。根据后文"break into the system, they might control your home or steal your personal information"（入侵系统、控制家居、窃取信息）可知，hackers指非法入侵计算机系统的"黑客"。括号内已给出中文释义"黑客"，也可根据上下文推断。【技巧】词义猜测要通过上下文逻辑：break into（闯入）+ steal（偷窃）→ 非法入侵者→黑客。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Smart homes are becoming more popular in modern cities. A smart home uses technology to make life easier and more comfortable. For example, you can control the lights, temperature, and even the refrigerator with your smartphone (智能手机).

One amazing feature is the smart assistant. It can understand your voice commands. If you say "It's hot in here," the assistant will automatically turn on the air conditioner. Some systems can even learn your habits. They know when you usually get up, so they prepare your coffee and open the curtains at the right time.

However, smart homes also have challenges. The equipment is expensive, and not everyone can afford it. Also, some people worry about privacy. If hackers (黑客) break into the system, they might control your home or steal your personal information.

Despite these concerns, experts believe smart homes will become common in the future. As technology improves and costs drop, more families will enjoy the convenience of smart living.
questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: B. How to Save Money at Home
C. Smart Homes: Convenience and Challenges
D. The Importance of Privacy Protection
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意题。文章第一段介绍智能家居的便利（convenience），第三段讲挑战（challenges：昂贵、隐私问题），最后展望未来。C选项全面概括了文章两方面内容。A选项只提到智能手机（只是例子）；B选项未提及；D选项只涉及隐私部分，以偏概全。【技巧】主旨题要覆盖全文主要内容，警惕以偏概全的选项（只讲文章某一部分）。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you manage your time better.

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to complete. (18)______ This helps you focus on the most important things first.

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Put away your phone and find a quiet place. (19)______ If you study for 45 minutes, take a 5-minute break to stretch or drink water.

Finally, don't forget to review your day before bed. Think about what you accomplished and what you can improve tomorrow. (20)______ Remember, good time management is a skill that takes practice.
options: A. However, taking short breaks can actually improve your focus.
B. As a result, you will become more efficient day by day.
C. Then, mark the most urgent ones with a star.
D. In addition, try to finish easy tasks quickly.
E. This is a common problem for teenagers.
questions.explanation: 【解析】前文提到"压力大、时间少"，E选项"This is a common problem for teenagers"（这是青少年的普遍问题）承接上文，This指代前文情况，并引出下文的解决建议。其他选项都涉及具体建议，不适合放在文章开头的总述位置。【技巧】首段末句通常起承上启下作用，要总结前文现象并引出后文建议。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you manage your time better.

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to complete. (18)______ This helps you focus on the most important things first.

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Put away your phone and find a quiet place. (19)______ If you study for 45 minutes, take a 5-minute break to stretch or drink water.

Finally, don't forget to review your day before bed. Think about what you accomplished and what you can improve tomorrow. (20)______ Remember, good time management is a skill that takes practice.
options: A. However, taking short breaks can actually improve your focus.
B. As a result, you will become more efficient day by day.
C. Then, mark the most urgent ones with a star.
D. In addition, try to finish easy tasks quickly.
E. This is a common problem for teenagers.
questions.explanation: 【解析】前文讲"写下所有任务"，后文讲"帮助你首先关注最重要的事"，C选项"Then, mark the most urgent ones with a star"（然后，给最紧急的任务标星）承上启下，Then表示顺序，mark urgent与focus on most important对应。【技巧】注意连接词Then表示步骤顺序，与First呼应。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you manage your time better.

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to complete. (18)______ This helps you focus on the most important things first.

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Put away your phone and find a quiet place. (19)______ If you study for 45 minutes, take a 5-minute break to stretch or drink water.

Finally, don't forget to review your day before bed. Think about what you accomplished and what you can improve tomorrow. (20)______ Remember, good time management is a skill that takes practice.
options: A. However, taking short breaks can actually improve your focus.
B. As a result, you will become more efficient day by day.
C. Then, mark the most urgent ones with a star.
D. In addition, try to finish easy tasks quickly.
E. This is a common problem for teenagers.
questions.explanation: 【解析】前文讲"避免干扰、专注学习"，后文讲"学习45分钟后休息5分钟"，A选项"However, taking short breaks can actually improve your focus"（然而，短暂休息实际上能提高专注力）承上启下，However转折引出休息的重要性，与后文take a 5-minute break对应。【技巧】However表示转折，前文讲专注，此处转折讲休息，逻辑通顺。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students feel stressed because they have too much homework and too little time. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you manage your time better.

First, make a to-do list every morning. Write down all the tasks you need to complete. (18)______ This helps you focus on the most important things first.

Second, avoid distractions while studying. Put away your phone and find a quiet place. (19)______ If you study for 45 minutes, take a 5-minute break to stretch or drink water.

Finally, don't forget to review your day before bed. Think about what you accomplished and what you can improve tomorrow. (20)______ Remember, good time management is a skill that takes practice.
options: A. However, taking short breaks can actually improve your focus.
B. As a result, you will become more efficient day by day.
C. Then, mark the most urgent ones with a star.
D. In addition, try to finish easy tasks quickly.
E. This is a common problem for teenagers.
questions.explanation: 【解析】前文讲"回顾一天、思考改进"，B选项"As a result, you will become more efficient day by day"（结果，你会一天天变得更高效）表示结果，总结前文做法带来的好处，与后文"时间管理需要练习"衔接自然。【技巧】As a result表示因果关系，用于总结前文措施带来的积极结果，适合放在段落结尾。
questions.label: 短文填空
